data_flow_pipeline.entry_point: User invokes bat command with file paths or stdin input. The main entry point is in build/main.rs which constructs a Config object from command-line arguments and creates a Controller instance.

stages.responsibility: Parse command-line arguments, open input files or read from stdin, detect input type
stages.input: Command-line arguments, file paths, or stdin stream
stages.output: OpenedInput struct containing file handle, metadata, and InputReader

stages.responsibility: Load syntax definitions and themes. Detect appropriate syntax based on file extension, name, or first-line content.
stages.output: SyntaxReference and Theme objects for highlighting

stages.responsibility: Apply user configuration. Preprocess input: expand tabs, strip ANSI codes, handle nonprintable characters.
stages.input: Raw file content, Config with user preferences
stages.output: Preprocessed content, validated line ranges, style configuration

stages.responsibility: Apply syntax highlighting. Parse ANSI escape sequences. Generate line numbers, git changes, grid decorations. Apply theme colors.
stages.input: Preprocessed line content, SyntaxReference, Theme, style components
stages.output: Highlighted lines with ANSI color codes and styling information

stages.responsibility: Format highlighted content for terminal output. Handle wrapping, pagination, and pager selection.

data_flow_pipeline.exit_point: Output is written to stdout directly or piped through a pager (less, more, or built-in pager). The OutputType enum determines the destination.

controller.role: Orchestrates the entire data flow pipeline.

input.role: Handles all input acquisition. Opens files, reads stdin, detects encoding.

config.role: Represents user configuration from command-line arguments and environment.

assets.role: Manages syntax definitions and color themes.

syntax_mapping.role: Maps file extensions and names to syntax definitions.

printer.role: Core rendering engine with InteractivePrinter and SimplePrinter.

vscreen.role: Parses and manages ANSI escape sequences.

theme.role: Handles color theme selection and detection.

line_range.role: Parses and validates line range specifications.

diff.role: Integrates with git to detect file changes.

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Controller calls Input::open to open files or stdin
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: File path or stdin indicator -> OpenedInput with InputReader

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Controller queries HighlightingAssets for syntax and themes
Controller creates InteractivePrinter or SimplePrinter, calls print_line for each line
Controller creates OutputType from config paging mode
Printer calls preprocessor functions before highlighting
Printer uses EscapeSequenceIterator to parse ANSI codes

cross_module_interactions.data_passed: Line number, git changes -> Formatted decoration strings

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Assets uses SyntaxMapping to resolve file names to syntax definitions
Controller calls get_git_diff if diff mode is enabled
cross_module_interactions.data_passed: File path -> HashMap of line changes

cross_module_interactions.interaction: Controller validates and applies line range filters

extension_proposal.feature: Custom output formatter plugin system (HTML, Markdown, JSON export)
extension_proposal.required_changes: 1) Create output_formatter.rs with trait OutputFormatter. 2) Modify output.rs for formatter selection. 3) Add --format CLI argument. 4) Update printer.rs to emit structured output. 5) Modify controller.rs to apply formatter.
extension_proposal.integration_points: Between printer and output stages: after InteractivePrinter::print_line generates styled output, the formatter intercepts styled lines, transforms them, and writes to the final output destination.
